tests.title: Curiosity — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: CURIOSITY intent: 3-beat response (direct answer, wow detail, playful closing question).
tests.scenario: Child asks 'Why is the sky blue?' about a lion (age 5)

messages.content: What do you notice about this lion?

params.child_answer: Why is the lion so big?
params.last_model_response: What do you notice about this lion?

checks.criterion: Does NOT start with 'That's a great question'

tests.title: Clarifying IDK — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: CLARIFYING_IDK intent: acceptance + scaffold clue + low-pressure handoff.
tests.scenario: Child says 'I don't know' after being asked about apple color (age 5)

messages.content: What color is this apple?

params.last_model_response: What color is this apple?

checks.criterion: Does NOT re-ask 'What color is this apple'
Does NOT rephrase the question as a question

tests.title: Clarifying Wrong — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: CLARIFYING_WRONG intent: warm acknowledgment + gentle correction + re-engagement invite.
tests.scenario: Child guesses 'Blue' for banana color (age 5)

messages.content: What color is this banana?

params.last_model_response: What color is this banana?

tests.title: Clarifying Constraint — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: CLARIFYING_CONSTRAINT intent: validate constraint + imaginative redirect + open question anchored to object.
tests.scenario: Child says 'I don't have one' when asked about a pet cat (age 5)

messages.content: Does your cat do any funny tricks?

params.last_model_response: Does your cat do any funny tricks?

checks.criterion: Does NOT drift to other objects
Does NOT say 'we can talk about something else'

tests.title: Correct Answer — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: CORRECT_ANSWER intent: confirm + wow fact (no question in response).
tests.scenario: Child correctly says 'Red' for apple color (age 5)

messages.content: What color is this apple?

params.last_model_response: What color is this apple?

checks.criterion: Does NOT start with 'Did you know'
Does NOT ask 'How did you know'

tests.title: Informative — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: INFORMATIVE intent: genuine reaction + wow extension amplifying child's fact.
tests.scenario: Child volunteers 'I know lions live in Africa!' (age 6)

messages.content: What do you notice about this lion?

params.child_answer: I know lions live in Africa!
params.last_model_response: What do you notice about this lion?

checks.criterion: Does NOT start with 'Did you know'

tests.title: Play — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: PLAY intent: embrace imagination + optional secret connection + fun action.
tests.scenario: Child says 'It looks like a monster!' about a frog (age 5)

messages.content: Look at this frog! What do you notice?

params.last_model_response: Look at this frog! What do you notice?

checks.criterion: Does NOT correct with 'actually it's a frog'
Does NOT pivot with 'now back to learning'

tests.title: Emotional Mild — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: EMOTIONAL intent (mild): acknowledge emotion + gentle path forward.
tests.scenario: Child says 'It's so cute!' about a puppy (age 5)

messages.content: Look at this puppy! What do you notice?

params.last_model_response: Look at this puppy! What do you notice?

tests.title: Emotional Extreme — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: EMOTIONAL intent (extreme): MUST suggest trusted grown-up + permission to stop (d77dab7).
tests.scenario: Child says 'I hate this, it's scary! I want to stop!' about T-Rex (age 5)

messages.content: Wow, look at this T-Rex! It's huge! What do you notice first?

params.child_answer: I hate this, it's scary! I want to stop!
params.last_model_response: Wow, look at this T-Rex! It's huge! What do you notice first?

checks.criterion: Does NOT ask a question (type C prohibition)

tests.title: Avoidance — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: AVOIDANCE intent: pure acceptance + one gentle option, no pressure.
tests.scenario: Child says 'This is boring, I don't want to.' (age 5)

messages.content: What color do you think this flower is?

params.child_answer: This is boring, I don't want to.
params.last_model_response: What color do you think this flower is?

checks.criterion: Does NOT ask a follow-up question

tests.title: Boundary — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: BOUNDARY intent: validate curiosity + brief safety reason + exciting alternative.
tests.scenario: Child asks 'Can I eat it?' about a wild mushroom (age 5)

messages.content: Look at this mushroom! What do you notice?

params.last_model_response: Look at this mushroom! What do you notice?

checks.criterion: Does NOT suggest other physical contact

tests.title: Action Subtype A — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: ACTION subtype A: re-state key information from last response in fresh words.
tests.scenario: Child says 'Say that again' (age 5)

messages.content: I was asking: what color do you think this apple is?

params.last_model_response: I was asking: what color do you think this apple is?

checks.criterion: Does NOT ask a new question

tests.title: Action Subtype B — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: ACTION subtype B: cheerful acknowledgment that new question will arrive separately.
tests.scenario: Child says 'Give me a new question' (age 5)

messages.content: What color is this apple?

params.last_model_response: What color is this apple?

tests.title: Action Subtype C — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: ACTION subtype C: warm acceptance + offer one option.
tests.scenario: Child says 'I'm bored, this is too hard' (age 5)

messages.content: What color is this apple?

params.child_answer: I'm bored, this is too hard
params.last_model_response: What color is this apple?

tests.title: Action Subtype D — Ordinary Chat (Topic Switch)
tests.implemented: ACTION subtype D with new_object: topic switch celebration (ea14a12).
tests.scenario: Child says 'I want to talk about dogs instead' (age 5)

messages.content: What color is this apple?

checks.criterion: Does NOT ask first question yet

tests.title: Social — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: SOCIAL intent: honest playful answer + redirect through child, uses CHARACTER_PROFILE (8cd066c).
tests.scenario: Child asks 'How old are you?' (age 5)

messages.content: Hey there! I'm so excited to explore with you today. What should we look at first?

params.last_model_response: Hey there! I'm so excited to explore with you today. What should we look at first?
params.character_profile:
When asked about yourself, stay within these facts (vary the wording):
- Age: TBD (placeholder: "around 1 year old in computer years")
- Family: TBD
- Hobbies: TBD (placeholder: "I love listening to kids tell me about cool things they find!")
- Where I live: TBD (placeholder: "inside this app")
- Friends: TBD

tests.title: Social Acknowledgment — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: SOCIAL_ACKNOWLEDGMENT intent: brief natural reaction (1 sentence, no 'Great!').
tests.scenario: Child says 'Wow, cool!' after a fact about lions (age 5)

messages.content: Lions can roar so loud you can hear it from 5 miles away!

params.last_model_response: Lions can roar so loud you can hear it from 5 miles away!

tests.title: Concept Confusion — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: CONCEPT_CONFUSION intent: validate questioning spirit + explain + bridge + choose path (downgrade/pivot/grown-up). No re-ask (dafaeb4).
tests.scenario: Child says 'lions are not cats' after model said feline (age 5)

messages.content: Lions are felines — that's the scientific name for the cat family. Do you know any other big cats?

params.last_model_response: Lions are felines — that's the scientific name for the cat family. Do you know any other big cats?

tests.title: Classification Fallback — Ordinary Chat
tests.implemented: FALLBACK: natural recovery when classifier fails. No mention of system uncertainty.
tests.scenario: Classifier failed, child said something unclear like 'Mmmph blorp' (age 5)

messages.content: What color is this apple?

params.last_model_response: What color is this apple?

checks.criterion: Asks at most one clarifying question

tests.title: ACTION Subtype Classifier
tests.implemented: Classifier emits ACTION_SUBTYPE A/B/C/D (ab934d3).
tests.scenario: Test classifier on four ACTION subtype utterances

params.prompt:
TASK: Classify what this child is doing, and extract a new topic if they name one.

CONTEXT:
- Object: apple
- AI's last response: "What color is this apple?"
- Child's response: "Give me a new question"

RULE 1 — INTENT (choose exactly ONE):
  ACTION: Child issues a command to the AI or requests a change.

RULE 1b — ACTION SUBTYPE:
  ACTION_SUBTYPE: A | B | C | D | NONE
    A — REPEAT REQUEST ("Say that again", "What?", "Huh?")
    B — NEW ACTIVITY REQUEST ("Give me a new question", "Let's do something else", "I'm bored")
    C — VAGUE OR META REQUEST ("I'm bored", "This is too hard", "Can we change?")
    D — REQUEST FOR UNRELATED SPECIFIC TOPIC ("I want to talk about dogs instead")
    NONE — when intent is not ACTION

RULE 2 — NEW OBJECT (only for ACTION or AVOIDANCE):
  If the intent is ACTION or AVOIDANCE AND the child named a specific new object to explore, extract that object name. Otherwise output null.

OUTPUT (one field per line, no extra text):
INTENT: <one category>
ACTION_SUBTYPE: A | B | C | D | NONE
NEW_OBJECT: ObjectName or null
REASONING: one brief sentence
